title: Index
summary: Introduces the ambient 0, base, and infinity index types and formal normal coordinates used by component calculations.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Introduces the ambient 0, base, and infinity index types and formal normal coordinates used by component calculations.

mathematicalContent.heading: Formal boundary
mathematicalContent.body: This page describes exactly the declarations present in Ambient/FG/Index.lean; claims not represented by a declaration are not counted as verified.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: Ambient Index. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: inductive AmbientIndex (ι : Type u) : Type u

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: kronecker. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def kronecker [DecidableEq ι] (I J : AmbientIndex ι) : ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: kronecker self. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem kronecker_self [DecidableEq ι] (I : AmbientIndex ι) : kronecker I I = 1

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: kronecker zero inf. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Formal proposition: kronecker inf zero. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Formal proposition: kronecker zero base. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem kronecker_zero_base [DecidableEq ι] (i : ι) : kronecker zero (base i) = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: kronecker base zero. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem kronecker_base_zero [DecidableEq ι] (i : ι) : kronecker (base i) zero = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Data/interface: Normal Coord. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.

references.locator: Chapters 2–3, ambient space and normal form
references.note: Geometric source for the ambient bundle, homogeneity, normal form, and commutator calculations.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
